steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $13w$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Simplify inside the parentheses
steps.content: Combine the like terms inside the parentheses first:$$4w+3w=7w$$So the expression becomes $20w-7w$.

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $10w$: would require $20w-10w$, but we have $20w-7w$. Eliminated.
  • B) $13w$: equals $20w-7w$. Matches.
  • C) $19w$: would require subtracting only $w$, not $7w$. Eliminated.
  • D) $21w$: larger than $20w$, but subtraction makes the value smaller. Eliminated.
That matches Choice B — $13w$.
